identity.short_name: CRT exponent targets with a prime square and an auxiliary prime

problem_spec.summary: Build $A$ by CRT modulo $p^k q^2 \ell$ with $A \equiv 1+p \pmod{p^k}$, $A \equiv g \pmod{q^2}$, and $A \equiv h \pmod{\ell}$. Convert the three conditions $A^n \equiv 1 \pmod{p^k}$, $A^n \equiv g^r \pmod{q^2}$, $A^n \equiv h^s \pmod{\ell}$ into a coupled exponent system and solve it by gcd reduction and modular inversion.
givens: $p,q,\ell$ are pairwise distinct odd primes with $q>3$.
$g$ is a primitive root modulo $q^2$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=\varphi(q^2)=q(q-1)$.
$h$ is a primitive root modulo $\ell$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$.
$r$ is an integer with $0<r<q(q-1)$ and $\gcd(p^{k-1},q(q-1)) \mid r$.
$s$ is an integer with $0<s<\ell-1$ such that the combined exponent system is consistent.
$B$ is the least nonnegative integer with $B \equiv g^r \pmod{q^2}$.
$C$ is the least nonnegative integer with $C \equiv h^s \pmod{\ell}$.
$A$ is the least nonnegative integer with $A \equiv 1+p \pmod{p^k}$, $A \equiv g \pmod{q^2}$, and $A \equiv h \pmod{\ell}$.
ask: Determine the unique integer $N$ with $0 \le N < 1000$ and $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{1000}$, where $n_0$ is the least positive integer $n$ such that $A^n \equiv 1 \pmod{p^k}$, $A^n \equiv B \pmod{q^2}$, and $A^n \equiv C \pmod{\ell}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: supplies the prime-power modulus $p^k$ and the LTE order of $1+p$ modulo $p^k$

parameter(s).name: k
parameter(s).purpose: forces a nontrivial prime power modulus and yields the exponent kernel $p^{k-1}$

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: supplies the prime square modulus $q^2$ with unit group order $q(q-1)$

parameter(s).name: ell
parameter(s).purpose: adds an auxiliary prime modulus enforcing a third exponent congruence modulo $\ell-1$

parameter(s).name: g
parameter(s).purpose: rigidifies exponents modulo $q^2$ by turning $A^n \equiv g^r$ into $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$

parameter(s).name: h
parameter(s).purpose: rigidifies exponents modulo $\ell$ by turning $A^n \equiv h^s$ into $n \equiv s \pmod{\ell-1}$

parameter(s).name: r
parameter(s).purpose: target exponent for the $q^2$ constraint and solvability guard against the $p^{k-1}$ kernel

parameter(s).name: s
parameter(s).domain: integer $s$ with $0<s<\ell-1$ consistent with the combined system
parameter(s).purpose: target exponent for the auxiliary prime constraint

invariant(s).name: CRT_synthesized_unit
invariant(s).statement: $M=p^k q^2 \ell$ with pairwise coprime factors, so CRT gives a unique $A \pmod M$ from local residues.

invariant(s).name: prime_square_generator_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=q(q-1)$, then $g^n \equiv g^r \pmod{q^2}$ if and only if $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$.

invariant(s).name: auxiliary_prime_generator_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$, then $h^n \equiv h^s \pmod{\ell}$ if and only if $n \equiv s \pmod{\ell-1}$.

essential_assumption(s): The condition that $g$ is a primitive root modulo $q^2$ is required to avoid discrete logarithm ambiguity modulo $q^2$.
The divisibility condition $\gcd(p^{k-1},q(q-1)) \mid r$ is required for solvability of $n \equiv 0 \pmod{p^{k-1}}$ and $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$.
The choice of $s$ must be consistent with the arithmetic progression forced by the first two congruences.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The three congruences on $A^n$ translate to the exponent system $n \equiv 0 \pmod{p^{k-1}}$, $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$, $n \equiv s \pmod{\ell-1}$. The first two determine a unique arithmetic progression by gcd reduction, and the third becomes a linear congruence in the progression parameter. This yields exactly one arithmetic progression of solutions, hence a unique least positive $n_0$ and a unique $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{1000}$ with $0 \le N < 1000$.

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder
technique(s).purpose: construct $A$ modulo $p^k q^2 \ell$ from local residues

technique(s).name: LTE_valuation
technique(s).purpose: compute $v_p((1+p)^n-1)$ and force the exponent kernel $p^{k-1}$

technique(s).name: multiplicative_order
technique(s).purpose: translate power congruences into divisibility constraints on exponents

technique(s).name: gcd_based_reduction
technique(s).purpose: solve $p^{k-1}t \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$ by dividing by $\gcd$ and inverting
outputs: arithmetic progression for the first two constraints

technique(s).name: generalized_CRT_sequential
inputs: arithmetic progression from the first two constraints and the third congruence modulo $\ell-1$
technique(s).purpose: reduce the third constraint to a linear congruence in the progression parameter

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
theorem(s).role: existence and uniqueness of $A$ modulo $p^k q^2 \ell$

theorem(s).name: cyclic_group_exponent_equivalence
theorem(s).role: in a cyclic group of order $m$, $g^n=g^r$ if and only if $n \equiv r \pmod m$

theorem(s).name: linear_congruence_theorem
theorem(s).role: solvability criterion and reduction for $ax \equiv b \pmod m$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: In a cyclic group generated by $g$ of order $m$, equality of powers is equivalent to exponent congruence modulo $m$.

critical_trick(s): Use primitive roots modulo $q^2$ and $\ell$ to convert power congruences into linear exponent congruences.
Solve the exponent system by gcd reduction and a final linear congruence in a progression parameter.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: gcd computations and fast modular exponentiation via pow

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: CRT construction of $A$, primitive root verification, and solving exponent congruences

task(s).name: verify_instance
task(s).reason: check that $A$ satisfies the CRT residues, compute $B$ and $C$, solve for $n_0$ by congruences, and verify the three power congruences
